title: Three-Bean Chili
steps: Heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté onion and bell pepper until softened, 7 minutes.
Add garlic and spices; toast 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes, beans, broth, and maple syrup. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 45–60 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened.
Season to taste. Serve with cornbread, sharp cheddar, or sliced avocado.
notes: Better the next day. Freezes well for up to 3 months.

title: Tomatillo Salsa Verde
steps: Heat a comal or cast iron skillet over medium-high.
Char tomatillos, serranos, onion, and garlic until blistered, 8–12 minutes.
Peel garlic. Remove serrano stems (seeds optional for heat).
Blend everything with cilantro, lime juice, and salt until smooth.
Taste and adjust salt. Chill before serving.
notes: Roots in the foothills. Goes on everything — eggs, beans, tacos, rice.

title: Miso-Glazed Tofu

ingredients.item: extra-firm tofu, pressed and sliced 3/4-inch thick

steps: Press tofu under a weight for 20 minutes to expel water. Slice into slabs.
Whisk miso, mirin, sake, brown sugar, soy sauce, and sesame oil in a small bowl.
Coat tofu slabs with glaze. Marinate 15 minutes (or up to 24 hours).
Heat broiler. Line a sheet pan with foil and brush with oil.
Broil 6–8 inches from heat for 8–10 minutes until glaze caramelizes and edges char.
Garnish with scallion and sesame seeds. Serve over rice with steamed greens.
notes: Watch closely under the broiler — sugar burns fast. Reserve extra glaze for drizzling.

title: Groveland Zucchini Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 325°F. Grease two 8×4 loaf pans.
Whisk eggs, oil, sugar, and vanilla in a large bowl.
Stir in zucchini.
In a separate bowl combine flour, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt.
Fold dry ingredients into wet. Stir in walnuts.
Divide between pans. Bake 60–65 minutes until a tester comes out clean.
Cool in pans 15 minutes, then turn out onto a rack.
notes: August zucchini, August oven, August house.

title: Red Lentil Dal
steps: Combine lentils, water, turmeric, and salt in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce to simmer.
Cook 15–20 minutes, skimming foam, until lentils break down into a porridge.
While lentils cook, heat ghee in a skillet over medium. Toast cumin seeds 30 seconds.
Add onion; cook until golden, 8 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, and serrano; cook 1 minute.
Stir in garam masala and cayenne. Add tomato; cook until softened, 5 minutes.
Stir tempered onion mixture into lentils. Simmer 5 minutes more.
Finish with cilantro and lime. Serve over basmati rice with naan.
notes: The tempering (tadka) at the end is what makes it. Don't skip it.

title: Wild Mushroom Risotto
steps: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp oil in a wide pan. Sear mushrooms in batches until golden, 5 minutes per batch. Set aside.
In the same pan, add remaining oil. Sauté shallot until soft, 3 minutes.
Add garlic and rice; toast 2 minutes until edges turn translucent.
Pour in wine; stir until absorbed.
Add warm broth one ladle at a time, stirring constantly. Wait until each addition is mostly absorbed before adding the next. About 18–20 minutes total.
When rice is al dente and creamy, fold in mushrooms, remaining butter, parmesan, and thyme.
Season and serve immediately.
notes: PNW chanterelles in fall make this. Substitute dried porcini (rehydrated) any other time.

title: Banana Sweet Potato Real Food Fuel

ingredients.item: pureed roasted or steamed sweet potato

steps: Puree banana with sweet potato.
Add the salt and water and blend until smooth.
Add more water, 1 tablespoon at a time, to achieve desired texture.
Store up to 3 days in the fridge. Put in a flask or Ziploc bag for in-run whole-food fueling.
notes: Tired of overly sweet gels? A real-food endurance fuel for long runs and rides. Per serving (approx): 109 cal, 27g carbs, 311mg sodium, 514mg potassium.
